mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably in production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for appropriate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> This command adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> This command installs llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls the llm-d release.
> This command deletes the cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> This command backs up the current configuration.
> This command upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct roles assigned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API endpoints for model serving.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, ensure to update your configuration files to match the new API specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed, along with sufficient resources for the cluster.
